title: Deep audit: 4 bugs fixed in covariance, drawdown_analyzer, sector_rotation, technical_analysis

bugs_found: covariance_diagnostics() analyzed eigenvalues of the covariance matrix instead of the correlation matrix for Marchenko-Pastur signal/noise classification — MP theory requires correlation matrices (trace=N), producing wrong signal eigenvalue counts
analyze_drawdowns() searched the FULL price series for peak dates, picking future dates when the same price appeared after recovery — caused negative peak_to_trough_days durations
sector_rotation relative strength used positional indexing (iloc) on separately-fetched SPY and sector ETF series, comparing returns from different calendar dates when series lengths differed
technical_analysis.py had 5 bare except Exception:pass blocks silently swallowing all errors from TA indicators (ADX, Stochastic, Williams %R, ROC, ATR)
observation.gap_identified: Math/logic bugs in diagnostic and analytics services that produce subtly wrong results without raising errors

bugs_fixed: covariance.py: covariance_diagnostics() now converts raw covariance to correlation matrix before eigendecomposition, ensuring MP bound, signal/noise counts, and condition numbers are correct (was misclassifying 2 of 3 signal eigenvalues as noise in synthetic test)

drawdown_analyzer.py: analyze_drawdowns() now restricts peak date search to prices.iloc[:i+1] (past only), preventing future dates from being selected when the same price reappears after recovery (was producing negative durations like -45 days)

sector_rotation.py: compute_sector_rotation() now aligns sector ETF and SPY dates via index intersection before computing relative strength, ensuring both return calculations use the same calendar dates
technical_analysis.py: replaced 5 bare 'except Exception: pass' blocks with specific (ValueError, ZeroDivisionError) catches + debug logging, preventing silent swallowing of unexpected errors

limitations: Bug 3 (sector_rotation) test is more of a principle demonstration than a direct regression test since the network call is needed for full validation
Did not audit all 55 services — focused on 10 files with complex math
Did not add integration-level tests for the copula_tail Student-t dead code
assessment.self_critique: Could have found more bugs by reading crash_model.py and signal_engine.py which have the most complex interaction logic. The covariance bug was the highest-impact find — it affected all portfolio optimization diagnostics.

next_steps: Audit crash_model.py and signal_engine.py for subtle math/logic bugs
Add benchmark_returns parameter to risk_number when called from portfolio analytics (beta always defaults to 1.0)
Consider removing dead code in copula_tail._fit_student_t (lines 261-267)
Add date alignment to other services that fetch data from multiple yfinance calls
